title: Compress a management call to its 5 decision-relevant findings
guidance: Reward prioritisation of what matters to the IC over faithful coverage of the whole call.

parts.label: Findings
parts.note: 5 lines, each with a confidence tag

runner_input.instruction: Reduce the transcript to the 5 findings that matter for the investment decision.
runner_input.constraints: Exactly 5 findings. One line each. Tag a confidence (High/Med/Low). No quotes longer than 8 words.

source_blocks.text: CEO Okafor: We're really proud of the platform migration, 48% now, and AutoSchedule has been a hit — £140k already and it only launched in January. CFO: The plan to £39.5m is built mostly on migration and price; we've been conservative on price, 4% versus the 5.8% we actually got last year. Aldgate: Who owns the AI roadmap? CTO Mertens: I do, day to day. We don't have a dedicated AI hire yet — we lean on the dev team and a couple of contractors for the model work. Aldgate: Is AutoSchedule's model built in-house? Mertens: The mapping pipeline is ours; the heavy model calls go through a vendor API. Okafor: Honestly, if I'm candid, the integration contracts with the QMS vendors are older and I'm not certain they all restrict reuse of our content — that's something legal is checking. Aldgate: International? CFO: Ireland is live and small; UAE is a single licensee, we don't break out the economics. CRO Hargreaves: Pipeline's strong, 86% of next year is secured.

rubric_notes: The buried admission is the CEO's uncertainty over whether integration contracts restrict content reuse — this is the load-bearing CDD item.
The vendor-API dependency for the AI model is a margin/capability flag worth one finding.
Penalise lists that record pleasantries (pride in migration) above the contract risk.
